verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable diagnosis immediately: "The decrement. `i--` makes `i` go down"; B also answers first: "`i--` decrements the counter instead of incrementing it."
verdict.decisiveness: Tie: both commit to one fix, "Change it to `i++`" / "Change `i--` to `i++`".
verdict.fabrication: Both slightly assume missing negative-index properties: A says "logging `undefined` after the first element"; B says "`arr[i]` is `undefined` for every negative index."
verdict.actionability: A is a little easier to act on because it is shorter and gives the fix directly: "Change it to `i++`:"
